How It Operates Internally: Opens real payment channels automatically using a Lightning Service Provider (LSP). Incorporates efficient technologies like Taproot channels and splicing. Uses trampoline routing (pathfinding delegated to LSP).
Ideal For: Daily payments while maintaining full control of your keys with zero technical friction.
Can Send to L1 (On-Chain)?: Yes (via Splice-Out — shrinks channel, sends on-chain TX)
Can Receive from L1 (On-Chain)?: Yes (via Splice-In / Swap-In — on-chain TX absorbed into channel automatically)
Can Send via L2 (Lightning)?: Yes (Native — instant, via trampoline routing through LSP)
Can Send/Receive Ecash (Cashu/Fedimint)?: No (Can pay a Cashu Mint's LN invoice like any standard LN payment, but cannot hold Ecash tokens)

How It Operates Internally: Does not manage channels for you: acts as a 'remote control' to connect directly to your own home node (Umbrel, RaspiBlitz, etc.). Performs source-based routing using a full network graph — no LSP dependency.
Ideal For: Advanced users and sovereign node operators who manage their own liquidity.
Can Send to L1 (On-Chain)?: Yes (via node's on-chain wallet — direct on-chain TX)
Can Receive from L1 (On-Chain)?: Yes (via node's on-chain wallet — generates standard addresses)
Can Send via L2 (Lightning)?: Yes (Native — sovereign source-based routing, no LSP)
Can Receive via L2 (Lightning)?: Yes (Native — requires managing own inbound liquidity)
Can Send/Receive Ecash (Cashu/Fedimint)?: No (Can pay a Cashu Mint's LN invoice like any standard LN payment, but cannot hold Ecash tokens)

How It Operates Internally: Runs a lightweight embedded Lightning node on the device with LSP assistance for channel management and liquidity. Similar to Category 1 but with more user control.
Ideal For: Users who want own-node sovereignty with the convenience of LSP-assisted liquidity.
Can Send to L1 (On-Chain)?: Yes (via LSP splice operations — similar to Cat 1)
Can Receive from L1 (On-Chain)?: Yes (via LSP splice/swap — on-chain TX absorbed into channel)
Can Send/Receive Ecash (Cashu/Fedimint)?: No (Can pay a Cashu Mint's LN invoice like any standard LN payment, but cannot hold Ecash tokens)

Technology Category: 3. Hybrid On-Chain / Swaps (L1 with LN Connector)
How It Operates Internally: Your funds are always stored on-chain as UTXOs in a 2-of-2 multisig (device key + server key). When paying on Lightning, performs a Submarine Swap using a Swap Provider. For small amounts, fees are optimized via batched settlement. Neither party can move funds unilaterally. Emergency Kit allows full recovery without servers.
Ideal For: Users who prefer a unified on-chain balance and use Lightning occasionally.
Can Send to L1 (On-Chain)?: Yes (Native — funds are always on-chain UTXOs)
Can Receive from L1 (On-Chain)?: Yes (Native — generates on-chain address, funds stored as UTXOs)
Can Send via L2 (Lightning)?: Yes (via Submarine Swap — fees vary with on-chain congestion since every LN payment triggers an on-chain TX)
Can Receive via L2 (Lightning)?: Yes (via Reverse Submarine Swap — Swap Provider receives LN, settles on-chain to Muun's multisig)
Can Send/Receive Ecash (Cashu/Fedimint)?: No (Can pay a Cashu Mint's LN invoice via Submarine Swap, but cannot hold Ecash tokens)

How It Operates Internally: Exchanges Bitcoin for cryptographic IOUs (Ecash tokens) using blind signatures (BDHKE). The Mint holds the backing Bitcoin collateral and runs its own Lightning node. Tokens are bearer instruments stored locally — unlinkable to any transaction. Fedimint distributes trust across a federation of Guardians.
Ideal For: Mathematical privacy, absolute micro-payments, instant same-mint transfers with near-zero fees.
Can Send to L1 (On-Chain)?: Yes (Indirect — Melt tokens at Mint, Mint's LN node pays a Swap Provider, Swap Provider sends on-chain TX)
Can Receive from L1 (On-Chain)?: Fedimint: Yes (via federation multisig address). Cashu: No (must deposit via Lightning first)
Can Send via L2 (Lightning)?: Yes (via Mint — Melt tokens, Mint's LN node pays the invoice)
Can Receive via L2 (Lightning)?: Yes (via Mint — Mint's LN node receives payment, mints new Ecash tokens for user)
Can Send/Receive Ecash (Cashu/Fedimint)?: Yes (Native within same Mint — instant bearer token transfer via NUT-18. Cross-Mint requires LN bridge)

How It Operates Internally: A company manages all nodes, channels, and keys on its own servers. In your app you only see a balance reflection (an internal IOU in their database). Intra-company transfers are pure database updates — no blockchain or Lightning involved. Note: some custodial wallets (e.g. Wallet of Satoshi) have introduced optional self-custody modes.
Main Wallets: Wallet of Satoshi, Strike, Satoshi Tango
Ideal For: Absolute beginners who want to experience Lightning speed without configuring anything.
Can Send to L1 (On-Chain)?: Yes (Company broadcasts on-chain TX from its hot wallet)
Can Receive from L1 (On-Chain)?: Yes (Company provides a deposit address — funds go to the company's wallet, user balance credited internally)
Can Send via L2 (Lightning)?: Yes (Company's LN nodes pay the invoice on user's behalf)
Can Receive via L2 (Lightning)?: Yes (Company's LN nodes receive payment, user balance credited internally)
Can Send/Receive Ecash (Cashu/Fedimint)?: No (Can pay a Cashu Mint's LN invoice via company's LN node, but cannot hold Ecash tokens)
